name: How can I tell if my new windows were installed correctly?
headline: How can I tell if my new windows were installed correctly?

mainEntity.name: How can I tell if my new windows were installed correctly or if it was a bad installation?
mainEntity.text: How can I tell if my new windows were installed correctly or if it was a bad installation?

acceptedAnswer.text: Signs of a **bad window installation** include drafts, difficulty opening or closing the window, visible gaps, uneven caulk lines, and water stains around the frame. If the window doesn’t sit square, latches don’t line up, or there’s movement when you push on the frame, those are red flags. Over time, you may also see peeling paint, swelling wood, or mold near the window. After installation, walk around your home in Pacifica, Daly City, or San Bruno and check each window. Do they open and close smoothly? Are the locks easy to use and secure? Look for consistent caulking and flashing details—nothing should look rushed or messy. On a windy or rainy day, pay attention to any whistling sounds, water leaks, or cold spots. A common mistake is assuming that, because the window looks nice from the inside, the job was done correctly. Much of the important work happens in the wall and exterior. Poor flashing or missing waterproofing details might not show immediately but can cause serious problems later. If you suspect a bad install, take photos and contact the installer right away. Most reputable companies will come back to adjust or fix issues. Don’t wait until water damage spreads; early attention can prevent bigger repairs.
